T1566.name: Phishing
T1566.description: Adversaries send phishing messages to gain access to victim systems via malicious attachments, links, or social engineering.
T1566.detection_approach: Email header and envelope analysis, URL sandboxing, attachment detonation, behavioral sender profiling, DMARC/DKIM/SPF validation
T1566.prevention_approach: Secure email gateway filtering, link rewriting and time-of-click analysis, attachment sandboxing, user awareness training

T1190.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
T1190.description: Adversaries exploit vulnerabilities in internet-facing applications such as web servers, VPNs, or firewalls to gain initial access.
T1190.detection_approach: Web application scanning, exploit attempt signature detection, anomalous HTTP request patterns, vulnerability correlation
T1190.prevention_approach: Timely patching, WAF rules, virtual patching, application hardening, attack surface reduction

T1133.name: External Remote Services
T1133.description: Adversaries leverage external-facing remote services like VPN, RDP, or Citrix to gain initial access using valid credentials.
T1133.detection_approach: Unusual remote access patterns, impossible travel detection, off-hours VPN connections, MFA failure spikes
T1133.prevention_approach: ZTNA replacement of VPN, MFA enforcement, conditional access policies, geo-blocking, device posture verification

T1078.name: Valid Accounts
T1078.description: Adversaries use compromised legitimate credentials to gain initial access, maintain persistence, or escalate privileges.
T1078.detection_approach: Impossible travel analysis, behavioral baselining, MFA challenge anomalies, concurrent session detection, dormant account usage
T1078.prevention_approach: MFA enforcement, conditional access, credential monitoring, privileged access management, password rotation

T1195.name: Supply Chain Compromise
T1195.description: Adversaries manipulate products or delivery mechanisms of software/hardware supply chain to compromise downstream targets.
T1195.detection_approach: Software composition analysis, dependency vulnerability scanning, build integrity verification, behavioral anomaly in updated components
T1195.prevention_approach: SCA scanning, SBOM generation, code signing verification, vendor risk assessment, build pipeline hardening

T1199.name: Trusted Relationship
T1199.description: Adversaries abuse trusted third-party relationships to gain access, such as IT service providers, MSPs, or supply chain partners.
T1199.detection_approach: Third-party access monitoring, unusual service account behavior, anomalous data access patterns from partner connections
T1199.prevention_approach: Zero trust segmentation for third-party access, JIT access provisioning, session monitoring, network segmentation

T1059.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
T1059.description: Adversaries abuse command-line interfaces and script interpreters like PowerShell, cmd, Bash, Python, or VBScript to execute commands.
T1059.detection_approach: Script block logging, AMSI integration, behavioral command analysis, process tree monitoring, encoded command detection
T1059.prevention_approach: Application whitelisting, constrained language mode, script execution policies, EDR behavioral blocking

T1204.name: User Execution
T1204.description: Adversaries rely on users to execute malicious payloads, such as opening weaponized documents, clicking links, or running programs.
T1204.detection_approach: Post-click behavioral monitoring, macro execution detection, child process analysis from document applications
T1204.prevention_approach: Email link protection, macro blocking policies, security awareness training, sandbox detonation, ASR rules

T1053.name: Scheduled Task/Job
T1053.description: Adversaries abuse task scheduling functionality to execute malicious code at system startup or on a scheduled basis for persistence.
T1053.detection_approach: Scheduled task creation monitoring, registry watching, cron job auditing, anomalous task properties
T1053.prevention_approach: EDR behavioral rules, application control, task scheduler ACL hardening, audit policy enforcement

T1047.name: Windows Management Instrumentation
T1047.description: Adversaries abuse WMI to execute commands, gather information, or move laterally within Windows environments.
T1047.detection_approach: WMI event subscription monitoring, WMI process creation tracking, anomalous WMI provider activity
T1047.prevention_approach: WMI namespace access controls, EDR monitoring, disabling remote WMI where unnecessary

T1547.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution
T1547.description: Adversaries configure system settings to automatically execute programs during boot or logon for persistence via Run keys, startup folders, etc.
T1547.detection_approach: Registry Run key monitoring, startup folder watching, service creation alerts, FIM on autostart locations
T1547.prevention_approach: EDR auto-remediation, registry protection rules, application whitelisting, least privilege enforcement

T1136.name: Create Account
T1136.description: Adversaries create new accounts (local, domain, or cloud) to maintain persistent access to compromised systems.
T1136.detection_approach: Account creation event monitoring, unusual admin account provisioning, cloud IAM change alerts
T1136.prevention_approach: Restrict account creation privileges, approval workflows, identity governance, automated deprovisioning

T1543.name: Create or Modify System Process
T1543.description: Adversaries create or modify system-level processes such as Windows services or systemd units to maintain persistence.
T1543.detection_approach: Service creation/modification alerts, systemd unit monitoring, driver installation tracking, FIM on service binaries
T1543.prevention_approach: Service configuration ACLs, code signing enforcement, EDR behavioral blocking, driver signing requirements

T1098.name: Account Manipulation
T1098.description: Adversaries manipulate existing accounts by modifying credentials, permissions, or group memberships to maintain access.
T1098.detection_approach: Permission change auditing, group membership modification alerts, service principal credential changes, OAuth consent grants
T1098.prevention_approach: Access reviews, PIM/PAM controls, alerting on admin role changes, principle of least privilege

T1068.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
T1068.description: Adversaries exploit software vulnerabilities to escalate privileges, such as kernel exploits or application-level privilege escalation bugs.
T1068.detection_approach: Exploit behavior detection, unexpected privilege transitions, kernel exploit indicators, anomalous system call patterns
T1068.prevention_approach: Timely patching, exploit protection controls, ASLR/DEP enforcement, kernel hardening, EDR exploit prevention

T1548.name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism
T1548.description: Adversaries bypass elevation controls like UAC, sudo, or setuid to gain higher-level permissions without proper authorization.
T1548.detection_approach: UAC bypass detection, sudo abuse monitoring, setuid binary anomalies, token manipulation detection
T1548.prevention_approach: Endpoint privilege management, least-privilege enforcement, UAC hardening, sudo configuration review

T1134.name: Access Token Manipulation
T1134.description: Adversaries manipulate access tokens to operate under a different user or system security context for privilege escalation.
T1134.detection_approach: Token impersonation detection, unexpected security context changes, process token analysis, logon event correlation
T1134.prevention_approach: Credential Guard, restricted token usage, least privilege, Protected Users security group

T1027.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
T1027.description: Adversaries obfuscate payloads, scripts, or data using encoding, encryption, or packing to evade signature-based detection.
T1027.detection_approach: Entropy analysis, deobfuscation engines, AMSI-based script inspection, behavioral analysis of decoded content
T1027.prevention_approach: ML-based detection, sandbox detonation, AMSI integration, script block logging analysis

T1070.name: Indicator Removal
T1070.description: Adversaries delete or modify artifacts to remove evidence of their presence, including clearing logs, deleting files, and timestomping.
T1070.detection_approach: Log integrity monitoring, event log gap detection, file deletion alerts, timestamp inconsistency analysis
T1070.prevention_approach: Centralized immutable log forwarding, FIM on log files, protected audit pipelines, tamper-proof logging

T1562.name: Impair Defenses
T1562.description: Adversaries disable or modify security tools, logging, firewall rules, or other defensive mechanisms to avoid detection.
T1562.detection_approach: Tamper protection alerts, agent health monitoring, security service status checks, EDR heartbeat monitoring
T1562.prevention_approach: Anti-tamper mechanisms, kernel-level agent protection, out-of-band monitoring, watchdog processes

T1036.name: Masquerading
T1036.description: Adversaries disguise malicious artifacts by manipulating names, locations, or metadata to appear legitimate.
T1036.detection_approach: Binary metadata verification, digital signature validation, path anomaly detection, hash comparison against known-good
T1036.prevention_approach: Application whitelisting, code signing enforcement, file reputation checking, behavioral analysis

T1112.name: Modify Registry
T1112.description: Adversaries modify the Windows Registry to hide configuration data, persist, or disable security features.
T1112.detection_approach: Registry modification monitoring, Sysmon Event ID 13, FIM on critical registry hives, behavioral correlation
T1112.prevention_approach: Registry ACLs, application control, EDR registry protection rules, GPO enforcement

T1003.name: OS Credential Dumping
T1003.description: Adversaries dump credentials from OS credential stores including LSASS memory, SAM database, NTDS.dit, or /etc/shadow.
T1003.detection_approach: LSASS access monitoring, credential dumping tool signatures, memory scanning, DCSync replication detection
T1003.prevention_approach: Credential Guard, LSASS PPL protection, privileged access workstations, disabling WDigest

T1110.name: Brute Force
T1110.description: Adversaries use brute force, password spraying, or credential stuffing to guess passwords or crack password hashes.
T1110.detection_approach: Failed login velocity analysis, password spray detection across accounts, credential stuffing patterns, lockout event spikes
T1110.prevention_approach: Account lockout policies, MFA enforcement, rate limiting, credential monitoring, banned password lists

T1558.name: Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets
T1558.description: Adversaries steal or forge Kerberos tickets (golden/silver tickets, Kerberoasting) to authenticate as any user without passwords.
T1558.detection_approach: Kerberoasting detection via anomalous TGS requests, golden ticket indicators, unusual Kerberos encryption downgrade
T1558.prevention_approach: Strong service account passwords, AES-only Kerberos, KRBTGT rotation, managed service accounts

T1552.name: Unsecured Credentials
T1552.description: Adversaries search for credentials stored insecurely in files, registries, code repositories, or cloud metadata services.
T1552.detection_approach: Secret scanning in repos, cloud metadata access monitoring, registry credential search detection, file access auditing
T1552.prevention_approach: Secrets management, credential scanning in CI/CD, environment variable encryption, IMDS v2 enforcement

T1556.name: Modify Authentication Process
T1556.description: Adversaries modify authentication mechanisms such as password filters, pluggable auth modules, or MFA to enable persistent access.
T1556.detection_approach: Authentication provider modification alerts, DLL loading in LSASS, PAM module changes, MFA configuration auditing
T1556.prevention_approach: FIM on authentication modules, LSASS protection, authentication provider hardening, change management

T1087.name: Account Discovery
T1087.description: Adversaries enumerate accounts on systems or in directories to identify targets for privilege escalation or lateral movement.
T1087.detection_approach: Unusual LDAP enumeration queries, net user/group command monitoring, API access pattern anomalies, directory query spikes
T1087.prevention_approach: Limit enumeration permissions, segment directory access, honeypot accounts, behavioral monitoring

T1082.name: System Information Discovery
T1082.description: Adversaries gather detailed system information including OS version, hardware, patches, and configuration to plan further actions.
T1082.detection_approach: Reconnaissance command detection (systeminfo, uname), WMI query monitoring, rapid sequential discovery commands
T1082.prevention_approach: EDR behavioral monitoring, reducing unnecessary system information exposure, process monitoring

T1046.name: Network Service Discovery
T1046.description: Adversaries scan for running services on remote hosts to identify exploitable services and map the internal network.
T1046.detection_approach: Internal port scan detection, service enumeration patterns, rapid connection attempts across hosts, Zeek conn logs
T1046.prevention_approach: Network segmentation, host-based firewalls, honeypots, limiting unnecessary service exposure

T1135.name: Network Share Discovery
T1135.description: Adversaries enumerate network shares to identify data stores, shared resources, and lateral movement opportunities.
T1135.detection_approach: SMB share enumeration detection, net share/view command monitoring, unusual share access patterns
T1135.prevention_approach: Share access controls, network segmentation, monitoring share enumeration, disabling unnecessary shares

T1021.name: Remote Services
T1021.description: Adversaries use remote services like RDP, SSH, SMB/Windows Admin Shares, or WinRM to move laterally within a network.
T1021.detection_approach: Anomalous lateral RDP/SSH sessions, first-time remote service usage, unusual authentication patterns across systems
T1021.prevention_approach: Network segmentation, ZTNA enforcement, jump server requirements, MFA for remote services, session monitoring

T1570.name: Lateral Tool Transfer
T1570.description: Adversaries transfer tools or files between systems within a compromised network to stage attacks on additional targets.
T1570.detection_approach: Unusual file transfer patterns between internal hosts, SMB write activity to remote systems, PsExec usage detection
T1570.prevention_approach: Network segmentation, application whitelisting, SMB signing enforcement, monitoring internal file transfers

T1080.name: Taint Shared Content
T1080.description: Adversaries deliver malicious content by adding it to shared network drives or repositories that other users access.
T1080.detection_approach: FIM on shared drives, malicious file creation monitoring, rapid file modification in shared locations
T1080.prevention_approach: Share access controls, real-time scanning of shared content, write restrictions, behavioral monitoring

T1563.name: Remote Service Session Hijacking
T1563.description: Adversaries hijack existing remote service sessions (RDP, SSH) to move laterally without needing additional credentials.
T1563.detection_approach: RDP session switch detection, tscon usage monitoring, SSH session anomalies, unexpected session handoff
T1563.prevention_approach: Session isolation through PAM, disconnect inactive sessions, RDP session security, credential separation

T1005.name: Data from Local System
T1005.description: Adversaries collect sensitive data from local system sources including file systems, databases, and configuration files.
T1005.detection_approach: Bulk file access monitoring, sensitive file read patterns, unusual data staging activity, clipboard monitoring
T1005.prevention_approach: DLP endpoint policies, file access controls, data classification, behavioral monitoring for staging

T1039.name: Data from Network Shared Drive
T1039.description: Adversaries collect data from shared network drives and file servers accessible within the compromised environment.
T1039.detection_approach: Abnormal volume of file reads from shares, unusual share access by user, bulk data enumeration patterns
T1039.prevention_approach: Share access controls, DLP policies on file servers, data classification, access monitoring and alerting

T1114.name: Email Collection
T1114.description: Adversaries collect emails from local clients, mail servers, or cloud email services for intelligence gathering or data theft.
T1114.detection_approach: Unusual mailbox access patterns, email forwarding rule creation, bulk email export detection, OAuth app email access
T1114.prevention_approach: Email access monitoring, forwarding rule alerting, OAuth consent controls, mailbox auditing

T1119.name: Automated Collection
T1119.description: Adversaries use automated techniques or scripts to collect internal data such as documents, credentials, or system information.
T1119.detection_approach: Scripted file enumeration and collection detection, rapid sequential file access, data staging patterns
T1119.prevention_approach: DLP policies, behavioral monitoring for bulk operations, script execution controls, data access limits

T1071.name: Application Layer Protocol
T1071.description: Adversaries use common application layer protocols like HTTP/S, DNS, or SMTP to communicate with C2 infrastructure to blend in.
T1071.detection_approach: Protocol anomaly detection, JA3/JA4 TLS fingerprinting, DNS analytics, HTTP beaconing detection, traffic pattern analysis
T1071.prevention_approach: SSL inspection, DNS filtering, application-aware firewall rules, web proxy enforcement, protocol whitelisting

T1105.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
T1105.description: Adversaries transfer tools or files from external systems into the compromised environment via C2 channels or alternate protocols.
T1105.detection_approach: Unusual download patterns, executable downloads from rare domains, certutil/bitsadmin abuse, PowerShell download cradles
T1105.prevention_approach: Web filtering, application control, download restrictions, SSL inspection, behavioral monitoring

T1572.name: Protocol Tunneling
T1572.description: Adversaries tunnel network communications over legitimate protocols like DNS, HTTP, or SSH to evade network security controls.
T1572.detection_approach: DNS tunneling detection, HTTP tunnel identification, unusual protocol payload sizes, entropy analysis of protocol data
T1572.prevention_approach: Deep packet inspection, DNS monitoring and filtering, proxy enforcement, protocol validation

T1090.name: Proxy
T1090.description: Adversaries use proxy connections to route C2 traffic through intermediary systems, making attribution and detection harder.
T1090.detection_approach: Multi-hop connection detection, Tor exit node communication, unusual proxy configurations, relay traffic patterns
T1090.prevention_approach: Egress filtering, proxy authentication requirements, blocking known anonymization services, SSL inspection

T1041.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
T1041.description: Adversaries steal data by sending it over the existing command and control channel rather than an alternative channel.
T1041.detection_approach: Unusual outbound data volumes over C2 protocols, data size anomalies in beaconing traffic, encrypted payload analysis
T1041.prevention_approach: DLP policies, egress data limits, SSL inspection, network monitoring, data classification enforcement

T1048.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
T1048.description: Adversaries exfiltrate data using protocols different from the C2 channel, such as DNS, ICMP, FTP, or cloud storage APIs.
T1048.detection_approach: DNS exfiltration detection, ICMP payload analysis, unusual protocol usage for data transfer, cloud storage API monitoring
T1048.prevention_approach: Protocol-specific egress filtering, DLP on cloud storage, DNS monitoring, ICMP inspection, proxy enforcement

T1567.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
T1567.description: Adversaries use legitimate web services like cloud storage, paste sites, or social media to exfiltrate stolen data.
T1567.detection_approach: Cloud storage upload monitoring, unusual SaaS data transfer volumes, personal account usage detection, paste site access
T1567.prevention_approach: CASB policies, tenant restrictions, DLP for cloud uploads, blocking personal cloud storage instances

T1486.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
T1486.description: Adversaries encrypt data on target systems to interrupt availability and extort victims, the primary ransomware technique.
T1486.detection_approach: Rapid file encryption patterns, canary file monitoring, file entropy analysis, VSS deletion detection, mass file rename
T1486.prevention_approach: Anti-ransomware behavioral rules, controlled folder access, automated endpoint isolation, backup protection

T1489.name: Service Stop
T1489.description: Adversaries stop critical services to disrupt availability, often as a precursor to ransomware deployment or data destruction.
T1489.detection_approach: Critical service stop event monitoring, bulk service termination detection, backup service disruption alerts
T1489.prevention_approach: Service protection rules, automated service restart, restricted service management permissions, behavioral monitoring

T1490.name: Inhibit System Recovery
T1490.description: Adversaries delete or disable system recovery features like VSS, backups, or boot configuration to prevent recovery from attacks.
T1490.detection_approach: VSS deletion detection (vssadmin/wmic), bcdedit modification monitoring, backup deletion alerts, recovery partition changes
T1490.prevention_approach: Protected backup infrastructure, VSS protection rules, behavioral blocking of recovery disruption, immutable backups

T1498.name: Network Denial of Service
T1498.description: Adversaries perform network denial of service attacks to degrade or block availability of targeted resources.
T1498.detection_approach: Traffic volume anomaly detection, protocol abuse patterns, application layer request rate spikes, botnet traffic identification
T1498.prevention_approach: DDoS mitigation services, rate limiting, traffic scrubbing, geo-blocking, CDN-based absorption

T1565.name: Data Manipulation
T1565.description: Adversaries manipulate data at rest or in transit to disrupt business processes, influence decisions, or undermine data integrity.
T1565.detection_approach: File integrity monitoring, database change auditing, transaction anomaly detection, data validation checks
T1565.prevention_approach: FIM, database activity monitoring, data integrity controls, access controls, backup and verification

T1505.name: Server Software Component
T1505.description: Adversaries abuse legitimate server software components like web shells, IIS modules, or SQL stored procedures for persistent access.
T1505.detection_approach: Web shell detection via file hashing, anomalous web server child processes, FIM on web directories, IIS module monitoring
T1505.prevention_approach: Application whitelisting on web servers, FIM on web roots, runtime protection, WAF rules, immutable infrastructure

T1569.name: System Services
T1569.description: Adversaries abuse system services like Windows Service Control Manager or launchd to execute malicious payloads.
T1569.detection_approach: Service execution monitoring, sc.exe usage tracking, launchctl monitoring, service binary path changes
T1569.prevention_approach: Service creation ACLs, application whitelisting, EDR behavioral monitoring, code signing enforcement

T1055.name: Process Injection
T1055.description: Adversaries inject code into running processes to evade defenses, escalate privileges, or access process memory.
T1055.detection_approach: Cross-process memory write detection, DLL injection monitoring, reflective loading detection, hollowed process identification
T1055.prevention_approach: Memory protection controls, attack surface reduction rules, EDR injection blocking, CFG enforcement

T1218.name: System Binary Proxy Execution
T1218.description: Adversaries use trusted system binaries (mshta, rundll32, regsvr32) to proxy execution of malicious payloads and bypass controls.
T1218.detection_approach: LOLBin execution monitoring, unusual parent-child process relationships, suspicious command-line arguments for system binaries
T1218.prevention_approach: Application control policies, ASR rules, blocking unused LOLBins, EDR behavioral detection

T1053.005.name: Scheduled Task
T1053.005.description: Adversaries abuse Windows Task Scheduler to execute programs at system startup or on a scheduled basis for persistence.
T1053.005.detection_approach: schtasks.exe execution monitoring, Task Scheduler event log analysis, XML task file creation, at.exe usage
T1053.005.prevention_approach: Restrict task creation to admins, audit scheduled tasks, EDR task creation blocking, GPO hardening

T1078.004.name: Cloud Accounts
T1078.004.description: Adversaries use compromised cloud account credentials to access cloud services, APIs, or cloud-hosted infrastructure.
T1078.004.detection_approach: Cloud-specific impossible travel, unusual API call patterns, service account abuse, OAuth token anomalies
T1078.004.prevention_approach: Cloud MFA enforcement, conditional access, service account monitoring, cloud access policies

T1059.001.name: PowerShell
T1059.001.description: Adversaries abuse PowerShell to execute commands, scripts, and download payloads, leveraging its deep Windows integration.
T1059.001.detection_approach: PowerShell ScriptBlock logging, AMSI inspection, encoded command detection, suspicious cmdlet usage, download cradle patterns
T1059.001.prevention_approach: Constrained Language Mode, script execution policies, AppLocker rules, PowerShell logging enforcement

T1003.001.name: LSASS Memory
T1003.001.description: Adversaries access LSASS process memory to extract credential material including NTLM hashes, Kerberos tickets, and plaintext passwords.
T1003.001.detection_approach: LSASS access monitoring via Sysmon Event 10, suspicious handle requests, known tool signatures (mimikatz, procdump)
T1003.001.prevention_approach: Credential Guard, LSASS as Protected Process Light, attack surface reduction rules, blocking debug privileges

T1021.001.name: Remote Desktop Protocol
T1021.001.description: Adversaries use RDP to interactively log into remote systems for lateral movement using stolen or legitimate credentials.
T1021.001.detection_approach: Anomalous RDP session patterns, first-time RDP connections, RDP brute force detection, unusual source-destination pairs
T1021.001.prevention_approach: NLA enforcement, RDP gateway through PAM, MFA for RDP, network-level RDP restrictions, session time limits

T1566.001.name: Spearphishing Attachment
T1566.001.description: Adversaries send targeted emails with malicious attachments designed to exploit vulnerabilities or trick users into execution.
T1566.001.detection_approach: Attachment sandboxing and detonation, macro analysis, exploit detection in documents, file type filtering
T1566.001.prevention_approach: Attachment sandboxing, macro blocking, file type restrictions, user awareness training, Safe Attachments

T1566.002.name: Spearphishing Link
T1566.002.description: Adversaries send targeted emails containing malicious URLs leading to credential harvesting pages or malware downloads.
T1566.002.detection_approach: URL reputation analysis, time-of-click URL scanning, brand impersonation detection, link redirect chain analysis
T1566.002.prevention_approach: URL rewriting and sandboxing, browser isolation, web filtering, Safe Links, DNS-based protection

T1071.001.name: Web Protocols
T1071.001.description: Adversaries use HTTP/HTTPS to communicate with C2 servers, blending malicious traffic with legitimate web browsing.
T1071.001.detection_approach: HTTP beaconing detection, JA3/JA4 fingerprinting, unusual User-Agent strings, domain reputation analysis
T1071.001.prevention_approach: SSL inspection, web proxy enforcement, domain categorization, allowlisting, behavioral traffic analysis

T1071.004.name: DNS
T1071.004.description: Adversaries use DNS protocol for C2 communications, encoding data in DNS queries and responses to bypass network controls.
T1071.004.detection_approach: DNS tunneling detection via query entropy, unusual TXT record requests, high-frequency DNS queries, DGA domain detection
T1071.004.prevention_approach: DNS filtering, protective DNS services, DNS query logging and inspection, blocking direct external DNS
